titleStmt.title: The Secretary of State to the United States Political Adviser for Germany ( Murphy ), at Berlin

div.head: The Secretary of State to the United States Political Adviser for Germany ( Murphy ), at Berlin
div.p:
385. Reurtel 394 August 30.Not printed. As stated in Dept’s 274 August 13, negotiations for interim agency for Rhine and Danube and the re-establishment of permanent agencies for international waterways will be considered by the Council of Foreign Ministers in London. In order not to prejudice these discussions, no agreement for a provisional organization should be reached by the LTS, British and French transport divisions of the Control Council and the problem should not be referred under any circumstances to the quadripartite transport
directorate or other riparian states. Current problems relating to resumption of traffic movement and navigation may however be worked out informally by the Control Council in cooperation with the Provisional Organization for European Inland Transport.For documentation on this subject, see pp. 1389 ff.
